name: Palworld
author: ops@refx.example
description: Pocketpair's creature-collection survival crafting game. SteamCMD-installed dedicated server with RCON support.
startupDetect: Setting breakpad minidump|Running Palworld dedicated server

installScript.script:
#!/bin/bash
# ReFx steamcmd shim — the steamcmd:debian/proton install image ships no
# `steamcmd` on PATH. Bootstrap SteamCMD and expose it on PATH so the egg's
# `steamcmd ...` calls resolve (otherwise the game server never downloads).
if ! command -v steamcmd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  mkdir -p /mnt/server/steamcmd
  curl -sSL https://steamcdn-a.akamaihd.net/client/installer/steamcmd_linux.tar.gz | tar zxf - -C /mnt/server/steamcmd || { echo "ERROR: failed to fetch steamcmd"; exit 1; }
  chmod +x /mnt/server/steamcmd/steamcmd.sh /mnt/server/steamcmd/linux32/steamcmd 2>/dev/null || true
  printf '#!/bin/sh\nexec /mnt/server/steamcmd/steamcmd.sh "$@"\n' > /usr/local/bin/steamcmd
  chmod +x /usr/local/bin/steamcmd
fi
set -uo pipefail
cd /mnt/server
# ---- Update / validate the game via SteamCMD -------------------------------
# A reinstall's job is to bring the game to the CURRENT build. The install runs
# as root (the runtime re-chowns the data dir to the game uid on start), so a
# genuine unix-permission wall is unlikely — SteamCMD's "Missing file
# permissions" here is almost always (a) stale partial-download state under
# steamapps/, or (b) the volume being out of free disk (SteamCMD misreports
# ENOSPC exactly that way). So: make the tree fully writable, attempt the
# update, and if it does not CONFIRM success, clear stale steam state + retry
# once. Crucially we do NOT claim success on failure — a silently-skipped update
# leaves the server on the OLD build, which clients reject as a "wrong build" /
# version mismatch on join. Customer world saves under Pal/Saved are never
# touched by any of this.
chown -R root:root /mnt/server 2>/dev/null || true
chmod -R u+rwX /mnt/server 2>/dev/null || true
STEAM_LOG="$(mktemp 2>/dev/null || echo /tmp/refx-steam.log)"
steam_update() {
  steamcmd +force_install_dir /mnt/server +login anonymous +app_update 2394010 validate +quit 2>&1 | tee "$STEAM_LOG"
  grep -q "Success! App '2394010' fully installed" "$STEAM_LOG"
}
if steam_update; then
  echo "[refx] Palworld is on the current build (SteamCMD confirmed)."
else
  echo "[refx] SteamCMD did not confirm the build — clearing stale download state and retrying…"
  df -h /mnt/server 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/[refx][disk] /' || true
  rm -rf /mnt/server/steamapps/downloading /mnt/server/steamapps/temp /mnt/server/steamapps/shadercache /mnt/server/Steam/appcache 2>/dev/null || true
  chown -R root:root /mnt/server 2>/dev/null || true
  chmod -R u+rwX /mnt/server 2>/dev/null || true
  if steam_update; then
    echo "[refx] Palworld updated successfully on retry."
  else
    echo "[refx] ERROR: SteamCMD could not update Palworld (app 2394010) after a retry."
    echo "[refx] The game files were NOT changed, so the server is still on its previous"
    echo "[refx] build and clients will see a 'wrong build' / version-mismatch error on join."
    echo "[refx] The most common cause is the server being out of free disk. Current usage:"
    df -h /mnt/server 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/[refx][disk] /' || true
    echo "[refx] Free up space (or upgrade the plan's disk), then reinstall again. Your world"
    echo "[refx] saves under Pal/Saved were left untouched."
    exit 1
  fi
fi
chmod +x PalServer.sh 2>/dev/null || true
# ---- Apply panel Startup-tab variables into PalWorldSettings.ini -----------
# Palworld reads gameplay settings from this ini and (silently) lets CLI flags
# override them, so we do NOT pass gameplay flags at boot; instead the panel
# writes the keys it owns into the ini here, at install/reinstall. Everything
# else in the ini belongs to the customer (file manager). Values are sanitized
# so they can't corrupt the single-line OptionSettings tuple.
INI_DIR=/mnt/server/Pal/Saved/Config/LinuxServer
INI="$INI_DIR/PalWorldSettings.ini"
mkdir -p "$INI_DIR"
if ! grep -q 'OptionSettings=(' "$INI" 2>/dev/null; then
  if [ -f /mnt/server/DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini ]; then
    cp /mnt/server/DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini "$INI"
    echo "[refx] seeded PalWorldSettings.ini from DefaultPalWorldSettings.ini"
  else
    printf '[/Script/Pal.PalGameWorldSettings]\nOptionSettings=()\n' > "$INI"
  fi
fi
D=$'\x01'
sstr()  { printf '%s' "$1" | tr -d '"[:cntrl:]'; }
snum()  { printf '%s' "$1" | tr -cd '0-9'; }
sbool() { case "$(printf '%s' "$1" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')" in 1|true|yes|on) printf 'True';; *) printf 'False';; esac; }
esc()   { printf '%s' "$1" | sed -e 's/[\\&]/\\&/g'; }
set_opt() {
  key="$1"; rep="$(esc "$key=$2")"
  if grep -Eq "[(,][[:space:]]*$key=" "$INI"; then
    sed -i -E "s${D}([(,][[:space:]]*)$key=(\"[^\"]*\"|[^,)]*)${D}\1${rep}${D}" "$INI"
  elif grep -q 'OptionSettings=()' "$INI"; then
    sed -i "s${D}OptionSettings=()${D}OptionSettings=(${rep})${D}" "$INI"
  else
    sed -i "s${D}OptionSettings=(${D}OptionSettings=(${rep},${D}" "$INI"
  fi
}
[ -n "${SERVER_NAME:-}" ] && set_opt ServerName "\"$(sstr "$SERVER_NAME")\""
mp="$(snum "${MAX_PLAYERS:-}")"; [ -n "$mp" ] && set_opt ServerPlayerMaxNum "$mp"
if [ -n "${ADMIN_PASS:-}" ]; then set_opt AdminPassword "\"$(sstr "$ADMIN_PASS")\""; else echo "[refx] AdminPassword: Startup-tab field empty — leaving PalWorldSettings.ini untouched"; fi
set_opt RCONEnabled "$(sbool "${RCON_ENABLED:-true}")"
rp="$(snum "${RCON_PORT:-}")"; [ -n "$rp" ] && set_opt RCONPort "$rp"
gp="$(snum "${SERVER_PORT:-8211}")"; [ -n "$gp" ] && set_opt PublicPort "$gp"
echo "[refx] PalWorldSettings.ini synced (panel keys: ServerName, ServerPlayerMaxNum, RCONEnabled, RCONPort, PublicPort${ADMIN_PASS:+, AdminPassword}); all other settings read from the ini as saved."
echo 'Palworld install complete.'

variables.description: Name shown in the in-game server browser. Panel-managed: written into PalWorldSettings.ini (ServerName) at install/reinstall.
Maximum concurrent players (hard cap of 32). Panel-managed: written into PalWorldSettings.ini (ServerPlayerMaxNum) at install/reinstall.

Password for in-game admin commands (/AdminPassword) and RCON. When set, it is written into PalWorldSettings.ini (AdminPassword) at install/reinstall; leave blank to manage it directly in the ini. To change it later, update this field and Reinstall (preserves your world), or edit the ini in the file manager.
Enable the remote console interface. Panel-managed: written into PalWorldSettings.ini (RCONEnabled) at install/reinstall.
TCP port for the RCON interface (allocated by the panel and written into PalWorldSettings.ini at install/reinstall).

longDescription: Co-op creature-collecting survival. Spin up a Palworld dedicated server for you and your friends in minutes.
